title: FINAL: Touch typing master
description: Final challenge — full English sentences at speed
text: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog and watches the sun rise above the city as morning begins
finger_focus: Full alphabet — pure speed and accuracy

tips.text: Here is the old fox again, this time with the sun coming up behind him. Every letter you own is in this line. Type it like a sentence you mean, not a list of keys.
Wrists level, fingers curved over the home row. That posture is what carries you when the page is long and the night is short.

tips.target: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog and
tips.hint: Full alphabet - pure speed and accuracy

tips.text: Breathe. The long words are no harder than the short ones; they only look that way. Keep the rhythm and they pass under your hands.

tips.title: From the editor
tips.text: The mark is twenty-five words a minute with fewer than seven slips. Hit it and the basics are behind you for good.

tips.target: watches the sun rise above the city as morning begins
tips.hint: Full alphabet - pure speed and accuracy

character_tips.anna: That is the whole alphabet, typed clean. The desk you started at is behind you now. Punctuation and numbers come next, and you are ready for them. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: You have the letters on your fingers, not in your head. That is the difference between a typist and a hunter. Well done. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Every key, in order, under speed. That is a finished hand. Take the win quietly and turn the page. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: You can set down any English word now without looking. Few people ever reach that. I am glad you did. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
